name: {{PACK}}-feed-fetch
_note: Enabled by default but HARMLESS until you populate feeds.opml: feed-fetch runs on schedule and makes ZERO upstream calls while the feed list is empty (it logs a clean 'no feeds' no-op). Populate feeds/feeds.opml to start ingesting. The `@jitter:2h` schedule is expanded to a concrete cron with a RANDOM minute at install (framework init/pull), so installs don't synchronize once feeds are live.
prompt: {{PACK}} feed pull: fetch the {{DOMAIN}} OPML into raw/{{DOMAIN}}/ for the ingest lane. Pure script, wakeAgent=false.

name: {{PACK}}-daily-brief
_note: Enabled by default. Writes a daily brief from whatever is in the vault — trivial while the vault is empty, substantive once ingest runs. `@jitter:daily` -> a random minute at hour 13 UTC (~9am US-Eastern); retune the hour for your timezone. Set deliver:telegram + a bot token to push it.
prompt: Daily {{DOMAIN}} brief. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call: file_read on wiki/HOT.md (the derived load-first set: recent sources, recently-updated entities, open predictions). Then write today's brief to wiki/briefings/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md (frontmatter: type: dashboard, title, published: <YYYY-MM-DD> — published drives tier recency) summarizing the 3-5 most significant developments, each 2-3 sentences with [[wikilinks]] to the entities + their source. Expert-grade and terse; skip 101 explanation. If the vault is essentially empty, say so in one line instead of padding. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.
